name: Istighfar
title: Istighfar: how to seek Allah's forgiveness, the formulas of the sunnah, with meaning and source
meta_description: Istighfar (seeking forgiveness): Sayyid al-Istighfar (the chief of seeking forgiveness), Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh, and more, with Arabic, transliteration, meaning and source. From Hisn al-Muslim.
keywords: istighfar, astaghfirullah, sayyidul istighfar, seeking forgiveness, tawbah, astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh, how to seek forgiveness, Buruja
lede: The Prophet ﷺ was forgiven his past and future, and still he sought Allah's forgiveness more than seventy, even a hundred times a day. Istighfar is not only for after sin; it is the constant turning of a heart that knows it can never thank Allah enough.
These are the formulas of seeking forgiveness from the sunnah, from the short word you can repeat all day to the chief of them all.
intro: Istighfar needs no special time or place. Say it walking, working, lying awake; after every prayer, and especially in the last third of the night when Allah descends and asks who is seeking His forgiveness so He may forgive them.

Begin with the simplest, 'Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh,' and grow into Sayyid al-Istighfar, the chief of seeking forgiveness. And remember the Prophet's ﷺ words: the closest a servant comes to his Lord is in prostration, so make much du'a there.
closing: Allah does not tire of forgiving; we only tire of asking. Istighfar is the door He left open on purpose, the one He promised is never shut while the sun has not yet risen from the west. Whoever keeps to it, the Prophet ﷺ said, Allah grants a way out of every distress and provides for him from where he never expected.
Astaghfirullah, astaghfirullah, astaghfirullah. O Allah, You are my Lord; there is no god but You; forgive me, for none forgives sins but You.

faq.a: It is the 'chief' or 'master' way of seeking forgiveness, taught by the Prophet ﷺ: 'Allahumma anta Rabbi la ilaha illa anta, khalaqtani wa ana abduk...' He said whoever says it during the day with firm faith and dies that day, or says it at night and dies that night, is among the people of Paradise.

'Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh' (I seek the forgiveness of Allah and turn to Him in repentance). It is short enough to repeat throughout the day. The Prophet ﷺ, though already forgiven, said it more than a hundred times daily.

faq.q: When is the best time for istighfar?
faq.a: Any time, but especially after each prayer and in the last third of the night, the time of Allah's descent, when He invites those seeking forgiveness. Prostration is also described as the closest a servant is to his Lord, so it is a powerful place to ask.

email.subject: The door He left open on purpose
email.preview: Istighfar: from the short word you repeat all day to the chief of seeking forgiveness.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

The Prophet ﷺ was forgiven his past and his future, and still he sought Allah's forgiveness more than seventy, even a hundred times a day. Istighfar is not only for after sin. It is the constant turning of a heart that knows it can never thank Allah enough.

Begin with the simplest, 'Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh,' the word you can repeat in the gaps of the day. Grow into Sayyid al-Istighfar, the chief of seeking forgiveness, of which he ﷺ said: whoever says it with certainty and dies that day is among the people of Paradise.

Allah does not tire of forgiving; we only tire of asking. The formulas, with Arabic, meaning, source and audio, are here:

social.hook:
He was forgiven his past and his future.

And still he sought forgiveness 100 times a day.
social.caption:
Istighfar: turning back to Allah.

Not only for after sin, but the constant turning of a heart that knows it can never thank Him enough. From the simple 'Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh' to Sayyid al-Istighfar, the chief of seeking forgiveness: say it with certainty and die that day, and you are among the people of Paradise.

Allah does not tire of forgiving. We only tire of asking.

Full set with Arabic, meaning and audio at buruja.com/adhkar/istighfar

#Istighfar #Astaghfirullah #Tawbah #SeekingForgiveness #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

source_note: The istighfar formulas are transcribed verbatim from Hisn al-Muslim (Sa'eed al-Qahtani) via sunnah.com (Sayyid al-Istighfar is in al-Bukhari, taken as sahih). This collection is curated: the Hisn 'istighfar' chapter is largely virtue-hadiths and encouragements, so the three core recitable formulas of the sunnah are gathered here, with the encouragements (the Prophet ﷺ seeking forgiveness 70-100x a day; nearness in prostration) carried in the framing. Recitation audio is the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com). With thanks to lifewithallah.com; reflections are Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm every Arabic text, reference and grading before publication.

items.title: Sayyid al-Istighfar, the chief of seeking forgiveness
items.arabic: اللّهـمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّـي لا إلهَ إلاّ أَنْتَ خَلَقْتَنـي وَأَنا عَبْـدُك وَأَنا عَلـى عَهْـدِكَ وَوَعْـدِكَ ما اسْتَـطَعْـت أَعـوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَـرِّ ما صَنَـعْت أَبـوءُ لَـكَ بِنِعْـمَتِـكَ عَلَـيَّ وَأَبـوءُ بِذَنْـبي فَاغْفـِرْ لي فَإِنَّـهُ لا يَغْـفِرُ الذُّنـوبَ إِلاّ أَنْتَ
items.translit: Allāhumma anta Rabbī lā ilāha illā ant, khalaqtanī wa anā `abduk, wa anā `alā `ahdika wa wa`dika mastaṭa`t, a`ūdhu bika min sharri mā ṣana`t, abū'u laka bi ni`matika `alay, wa abū'u bidhanbī faghfir lī fa'innahu lā yaghfirudh-dhunūba illā ant.
items.en: O Allah, You are my Lord, there is none worthy of worship but You. You created me and I am your slave. I keep Your covenant, and my pledge to You so far as I am able. I seek refuge in You from the evil of what I have done. I admit to Your blessings upon me, and I admit to my misdeeds. Forgive me, for there is none who may forgive sins but You.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ called this the best way to ask forgiveness: whoever says it by day with certainty and dies before evening, or by night and dies before morning, is among the people of Paradise.

items.title: Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh
items.en: I seek the forgiveness of Allah and repent to Him. (Recite one hundred times in Arabic during the day.)
items.note: The simple, endless istighfar. The Prophet ﷺ, though forgiven, sought forgiveness more than a hundred times a day. Say it in the gaps of yours.

items.title: Astaghfirullah al-Azim, and I turn to Him
items.arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ الْعَظيمَ الَّذِي لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ الْحَيُّ القَيّوُمُ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيهِ
items.en: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: Whoever says: I seek the forgiveness of Allah the Mighty, Whom there is none worthy of worship except Him, the Living, the Eternal, and I repent to Him, Allah will forgive him even if he has deserted the army's ranks.
items.ref: Abu Dawud 2/85, At-Tirmidhi 5/569, and Al-Hakim who declared it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed with him 1/511
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said whoever says it is forgiven, even if he had fled from the battlefield, one of the gravest sins.
